Title: Integrated network analyses identify MYB4R1 neofunctionalization in the UV-B adaptation of Tartary buckwheat

Pathway_Genes.Catalyzed_Reaction_Description: Condenses p-coumaroyl-CoA with malonyl-CoA to form naringenin chalcone, a central flavonoid precursor.
Pathway_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Function: Protection against UV-B damage; contributes to seed color
Pathway_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Expression changed during FtMYB4R1 transient overexpression and its promoter was identified as a direct FtMYB4R1 binding target via L-box motif in the study conclusion.
Pathway_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: qRT-PCR during FtMYB4R1 transient overexpression; promoter motif analysis; conclusion supported by yeast one-hybrid context and study summary
Pathway_Genes.Environment_or_Treatment_Factor: UV-B radiation; transient overexpression of FtMYB4R1 in seeds
Pathway_Genes.Applied_Promoters: Native promoter assayed for L-box motif targeting
Pathway_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. FtCHS is one of the flavonoid biosynthetic genes highlighted as a downstream target in the FtMYB4R1 regulatory mechanism. 2. Its promoter contains an L-box motif according to the study conclusion. 3. FtCHS participates in the upstream common branch supplying flavonoid/anthocyanin biosynthesis in seeds under UV-B-related regulation.
Pathway_Genes.Other_Important_Info: Y1H using the tested GTTAG-containing FtCHS fragment did not show binding; authors state this was likely because the L-box-containing promoter fragment was not used. The concluding text states FtMYB4R1 binds L-box motifs in FtCHS, FtFLS, and FtUFGT promoters.

Pathway_Genes.Catalyzed_Reaction_Description: Converts dihydroflavonols into flavonols in the flavonol branch of flavonoid biosynthesis.
Pathway_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Function: Protection against UV-B damage; contributes to nutritional rutin/flavonol accumulation
Pathway_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Downregulated upon FtMYB4R1 transient overexpression; directly bound by FtMYB4R1 at an L-box motif in its promoter.
Pathway_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: qRT-PCR during FtMYB4R1 transient overexpression; yeast one-hybrid; EMSA
Pathway_Genes.Environment_or_Treatment_Factor: UV-B radiation; transient overexpression of FtMYB4R1 in seeds
Pathway_Genes.Applied_Promoters: Native promoter assayed for L-box motif targeting
Pathway_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. FtFLS is a downstream flavonoid biosynthetic gene repressed in FtMYB4R1-overexpressing seeds. 2. FtMYB4R1 binds the L-box-containing FtFLS promoter in yeast one-hybrid assays. 3. EMSA confirmed direct in vitro binding of FtMYB4R1 to the FtFLS promoter.
Pathway_Genes.Other_Important_Info: The paper’s mechanistic evidence specifically supports FtFLS as a direct transcriptional target of FtMYB4R1.

Pathway_Genes.Catalyzed_Reaction_Description: Transfers glucose to anthocyanidins to form stable anthocyanins in the anthocyanin branch.
Pathway_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Downregulated upon FtMYB4R1 transient overexpression; directly bound by FtMYB4R1 at an L-box motif in its promoter.
Pathway_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: qRT-PCR during FtMYB4R1 transient overexpression; yeast one-hybrid; EMSA
Pathway_Genes.Environment_or_Treatment_Factor: UV-B radiation; transient overexpression of FtMYB4R1 in seeds
Pathway_Genes.Applied_Promoters: Native promoter assayed for L-box motif targeting
Pathway_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. FtUFGT is a downstream anthocyanin biosynthetic gene repressed in FtMYB4R1-overexpressing seeds. 2. FtMYB4R1 binds the L-box-containing FtUFGT promoter in yeast one-hybrid assays. 3. EMSA confirmed direct in vitro binding of FtMYB4R1 to the FtUFGT promoter.
Pathway_Genes.Other_Important_Info: The paper uses anthocyanin-related seed color changes as a functional readout linked to FtUFGT regulation.

Regulation_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Negatively regulates flavonoid accumulation and seed color; its expression is reduced by UV-B, which is associated with increased seed flavonoid content.
Regulation_Genes.Regulatory_Effect_on_Target_Genes: Represses downstream flavonoid/anthocyanin genes FtFLS and FtUFGT during transient overexpression; study conclusion indicates regulation via L-box motifs in FtCHS, FtFLS, and FtUFGT promoters
Regulation_Genes.Regulation_Mode: Transcriptional repression via promoter binding to L-box motifs
Regulation_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: qRT-PCR expression profiling; UV-B treatment; stable overexpression in Arabidopsis; transient overexpression in Tartary buckwheat seeds; yeast one-hybrid; EMSA; metabolite profiling; seed color imaging
Regulation_Genes.Decisive_Influence_on_Target_Product: Core regulator at the center of the UV-B/flavonoid co-functional network; neofunctionalized UV-B-responsive repressor controlling seed flavonoid and anthocyanin accumulation
Regulation_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. FtMYB4R1 was identified as the best-connected MYB at the core of the UV-B response/flavonoid biosynthesis network in Tartary buckwheat. 2. FtMYB4R1 is highly expressed in seeds and is significantly downregulated by UV-B treatment. 3. UV-B decreases FtMYB4R1 expression while increasing seed flavonoid content and changing seed color. 4. Stable overexpression in Arabidopsis and transient overexpression in buckwheat seeds reduce flavonoid content and alter seed color. 5. FtMYB4R1 directly binds L-box motifs in FtFLS and FtUFGT promoters, and the study concludes a similar mechanism for FtCHS. 6. Evolutionary analyses indicate positive selection and neofunctionalization of FtMYB4R1 in Tartary buckwheat.
Regulation_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Source_Species: Highly expressed in seeds; lower in stem, root, leaf, fruit, and flower relative to seeds
Regulation_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Applied_Species: Highly expressed in seeds; significantly reduced after 2 h UV-B treatment
Regulation_Genes.Applied_Promoters: CaMV35S promoter (constitutive) for Arabidopsis stable overexpression
Regulation_Genes.Environment_or_Treatment_Factor: UV-B radiation (0.2 mW cm^-2, 2 h); natural populations from low-, medium-, and high-UV-B regions
Regulation_Genes.Interacting_Proteins: FtFLS promoter; FtUFGT promoter; FtCHS promoter
Regulation_Genes.Omics_Data: Large-scale transcriptomics from >10,000 samples across >80 projects; DAP-seq; CF-MS; natural population expression/qRT-PCR; image-based phenotyping
Regulation_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Useful target for breeding or engineering Tartary buckwheat varieties with altered seed flavonoid content and color, and for adaptation-related selection under different UV-B environments; lower FtMYB4R1 expression is associated with higher seed flavonoids.
Regulation_Genes.Other_Important_Info: The paper emphasizes neofunctionalization relative to AtMYB4R1 orthologs; the mechanism appears distinct from previously known buckwheat MYB repressors such as FtMYB16. Applied species must be distinguished: mechanistic work was mainly in Tartary buckwheat, while heterologous overexpression validation was performed in Arabidopsis.
